subject: Mathematics
title: The Birth of Algebra
description: A short lesson to help you apply The Birth of Algebra.

hook.text: The word 'algebra' comes from Arabic. The entire field was born in 9th-century Baghdad.

buildup.text: Al-Khwarizmi wrote a book on restoring and balancing equations around 820 AD.

discovery.text: His techniques for solving linear and quadratic equations were revolutionary.

twist.text: The word 'algorithm' also comes from his name—he shaped two pillars of modern math.

climax.text: His work was translated into Latin and dominated European mathematics for 500 years.

punchline.text: One book from Baghdad gave the world algebra and algorithms.

quiz.question: Where does the word 'algebra' originate?
options: From an Arabic book by Al-Khwarizmi
